title: Ivan Rodden
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Ivan Rodden
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
biography: Ivan Rodden (aka Ivan Faute) is a graduate of The Program for Writers at the University of Illinois at Chicago. He primarily teaches creative writing courses in prose and playwriting. His creative work appears under the name "Ivan Faute" and has appeared in over three dozen literary journals and magazines including The Southeast Review, Harpur Palate, and The Pinch. His plays have been produced in New York, Chicago, Houston, and London.
education_entries: Ph D in English, University of Illinois
MA in English, University of Illinois
research_interests: Drama - African American playwrights and presentation Performance Theory Creative Writing Pedagogy Cultural representation and interpretation in creative writing
teaching_interests: Fiction Writing Playwriting Creative Writing Fabulist Writing African American Drama Creative Nonfiction Writing

Artistic Accomplishments: Theatre - Write play "Lost Sock Laundry". UP Theatre. UP Theatre. New York, NY.
Theatre - Write play "Whales Are Gossips". Island of Misfit Adults. Little Theatre of Norfolk. Norfolk, VA.
Theatre - Write play "Urban Gardening". Copenhagen Theatre Circle. Improv Comedy Copenhagen. Copenhagen, Denmark.
Theatre - Write play "Slaughter". William Inge Theater Festival. Independence, KS.
Theatre - Write play "The Dog Show". The Players Theatre. Sgourous Theater. New York, NY.
Drama - Book or Collection of Plays "Lavanderia Calzino Perso". OnStage! Festival. OnStage! Festival. Italy.
Theatre - Write play "On Arriving". Searchout Theatre. Cyprus High Commission. London, UK.
Presentations: Dan Rodden, PCA ACA Annual Conference, "Fanny, Buster, and Bertolt Walk on Stage: A Collaborative Experiment," Washington, DC. (2019)
Dan Rodden, AWP Annual Conference, "Furries, Fairies, and Fetuses: When Earnestness Derails the Short Story," Los Angeles, CA. (2016)
Grants and Sponsored Research: Ivan Rodden, "Konvent Writing Residency," Sponsored by Konvent de Cal Rosal
Ivan Rodden, "Fifty Years Later: The Vietnam War Through the Eyes of Veterans, Vietnamese, and Southeast Asian Refugees," Sponsored by Coastline College
